item.title: Caleidoscopio Juega y Aprende
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Monday, March 30, 2026, 10 - 11:30am Los niños escuchan cuentos multilingües, cantan canciones y hacen manualidades sencillas con sus cuidadores durante el tiempo en grupo. Este programa se realizará únicamente en español. Location Link: Delridge Branch Room Location: Sala de reuniones Registration Required: No se requiere inscripción. Detailed Description: El Caleidoscopio Juega y Aprende ofrece a los niños y sus familias la oportunidad de venir a jugar y pasar un buen rato., También hay un tiempo de juego para que los niños exploren diversos juguetes y actividades infantiles., Los niños aprenden y exploran el uso del arte, la música, los juguetes y mucho más. Las familias aprenden sobre el desarrollo de sus hijos y los recursos comunitarios que tienen a sus disposición., Agradecemos a nuestro socio Villa Comunitaria ., --------------------------------------------, Kaleidoscope Play & Learn offers a chance for children and their families to come play and have a good time., There is also a play time for children to explore various toys and play activities., Children learn and explore the use of art, music, toys and more. Families learn about their child&'s development and community resources available to them., Thanks to our partner Villa Comunitaria . Event Logistics: Este grupo está dirigido a niños de 0 a 5 años de todas las habilidades., Caleidoscopio Juega y Aprende es para familias, amigos, vecinos que cuidan niños, padres y los niños a su cargo.Este programa no está diseñado para dejar a los niños solos y no es adecuado para guarderías, centros preescolares u otras organizaciones dedicadas al cuidado de niños., Obtenga más información sobre los programas Aprendizaje temprano y la hora de cuentos ., --------------------------------------------, This group is open to children of all abilities, ages 0 to 5 years old.Kaleidoscope play and learn is for Family, Friend, Neighbor Caregivers, Parents, and the children in their care. This is not a drop-off program and it is not agood fit for daycare centers, preschools, or other childcare organizations., Find out more about Early Learning & Story Time programs. item.title: Free Tax Help
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 11 AM

Monday, March 30, 2026, 11am - 5pm Trained volunteers will be available to answer questions and prepare personal tax returns. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 5 - Mixing Chamber Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Drop-in tax help is offered on a first-come, first-served basis., This service is free and available to individuals only., The free service is not available for business tax returns., To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required Documents: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return, Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return, Recommended: Last year&'s tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents will be accepted., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov. , You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner United Way of King County . Event Logistics: United Way Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025 \(or less than $96,000 for non-single filers\)., For eligibility details, contact United Way at 206-461-3700. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.,

Monday, March 30, 2026, 11am - 5pm Trained volunteers will be available to answer questions and prepare personal tax returns. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 5 - Mixing Chamber Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Drop-in tax help is offered on a first-come, first-served basis., This service is free and available to individuals only., The free service is not available for business tax returns., To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required Documents: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return, Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return, Recommended: Last year&'s tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents will be accepted., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov. , You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner United Way of King County . Event Logistics: United Way Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025 \(or less than $96,000 for non-single filers\)., For eligibility details, contact United Way at 206-461-3700. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.,

Event details

item.title: Virtual Workshop - Become a Small Business Owner Through Franchising
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 1 PM

Monday, March 30, 2026, 1 - 3pm If you are considering franchising as a career option, or actively working towards a franchise agreement, this program is for you. Location Link: Online Registration Required: Registration is required. Detailed Description: This workshop is designed both for individuals at the early stage of considering franchising as a career option and those actively considering a franchise but who have not yet signed the agreement., Topics covered include: Understand what is and is not a franchise, Define a positive relationship with the franchisor, Laws and legal requirements that apply to franchising in Washington State, Important criteria in choosing a franchise, what questions to ask and how to ask them, Research hints and tools, A view into a method to segment franchises in six different categories, A discussion on financing, incorporation and preparation relating to a franchise award, Navigating the myriad of franchise opportunities available, Doing your due diligence, Narrowing down your search, What to consider and what matters to you, Answers to your specific questions, About the Presenter:, Jeff Levy is an educator, author and coach. Herelocated from NYC for a 3-person tech startup up in WA State, has managed up to 300 people across international borders and has been a franchisee since 2002. His personal experiences include raising capital, starting and acquiring businesses and investing in a franchise during a career transition. He has also served on multiple boards, committees and has contributed voluntary work on community projects., Registration is required. Register here . Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.

item.title: Lumen Field Stadium Tours
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 2:30 PM

Location: Lumen Field, 800 Occidental Ave S,, Seattle, WA

General Admission Only Tours depart promptly at their scheduled time. Please arrive early to facilitate security screening. If you arrive late, there are no guarantees tour guides will wait for you or your party. Tour guests will enter doors inside the Pro-Shop off Occidental Ave. Signs will direct you to the appropriate door. There is no extended field access on the tour route. All sales are final. There are no refunds or exchanges into future dates or times.

item.title: Homework Help
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Monday, March 30, 2026, 3:30 - 5:30pm Homework Help volunteers will be at this branch to assist K-12 students with school assignments during the school year. Location Link: Northgate Branch Room Location: Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: If you are working on a tough homework assignment or have questions about a lesson, come to the Library for Homework Help. We have volunteers who can help with math, English, social studies and science., Students without homework can read and play learning enrichment games with trained tutors., Snacks are provided at every Homework Help: Thanks to the donors of The Seattle Public Library Foundation for sponsoring this event. Event Logistics: , Registration is not required but these programs are very popular and the room capacity can fill up quickly., While Homework Help is available to students from kindergarten through 12th grade, volunteers are not responsible for children, who should not be left unsupervised., Find out more about Homework Help. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.

item.title: Mahjong Masters Free Play
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Stroum Jewish Community Center, 3801 East Mercer Way, Mercer Island, WA

New at the J! Mahjong Masters is a once-a-month free play social event for advanced and expert mahjong players. Bring friends, meet new ones, and grab a nosh at these exclusive sessions open only to the seasoned players in our community. We recommend that all participants be comfortable playing at a quick pace and have a complete understanding of the rules and game. These sessions are free but we ask you to register in advance

item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

item.title: Moomins' Sea Adventures & Tove and the Sea
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Preschool Story Time
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 10:30 - 11am At storytime, kids ages 3-5 and their families can enjoy stories, songs, and fun learning activities. Location Link: South Park Branch Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Early Learning is a great way for preschoolers and their families to develop learning, literacy, and language skills as well as community with other families., We read stories, sing songs, and have engaging, educational activities that help kids and their caregivers grow together. Event Logistics: Registration is not required but these programs are very popular and the room capacity can fill up quickly., Families of all ages are welcome, though this early learning program is intended for youth aged 3-5, their caregivers and families., Find out more about Early Learning & Story Time programs. item.title: Free Tax Help
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 11 AM

Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 11am - 3pm Trained volunteers will be available to answer questions and prepare personal tax returns. Location Link: Ballard Branch Room Location: Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: This service is free and available to individuals only. The free service is not available for business tax returns. To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return., Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return., Recommended: Last year&'s tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents will be accepted.Original documents highly recommended., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov ., You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner AARP . Event Logistics: AARP Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025., For eligibility details, contact AARP at 888-227-7669. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.

Event details

item.title: Matchmaking Your Business Build your network. Save time. Strengthen your resources.
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Mercer Island Community Center, 2247 84th Ave SE, Mercer Island, WA

Matchmaking Your Business is a structured, high-energy networking event designed to connect Realtors with trusted industry vendors. Location: Mercer Island Community Center 8236 SE 24th Street, Mercer Island, WA 98040 Parking: Free When: Tuesday, March 31st, 2026﻿ ﻿Time: 11am to 1pm Matchmaking Your Business is a structured, high-energy networking event designed to connect Realtors with trusted industry vendors. The goal is simple: Help Realtors build reliable vendor partnerships that elevate the client experience, streamline transactions, and support long-term business growth. Tired of calling vendor after vendor trying to find someone reliable for your clients? Come meet them in one place. Our WCR Seattle Metro Vendor Speed Networking Event gives you the chance to connect one-on-one with trusted industry professionals who can help support your business and your clients. Build your network. Save time. Strengthen your resources. Just real connections that build your book of business. Come ready to connect, collaborate, and fine the right matches for your business.

item.title: Free Tax Help
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 12:30 PM

Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 12:30 - 6:30pm Trained volunteers will be available to answer questions and prepare personal tax returns. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 5 - Mixing Chamber Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: This service is free and available to individuals only. The free service is not available for business tax returns., To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return, Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return, Recommended: Last year&'s tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents will be accepted., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov. , You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner United Way of King County . Event Logistics: United Way Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025 \(or less than $96,000 for non-single filers\)., For eligibility details, contact United Way at 206-461-3700. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.,

The free service is not available for business tax returns., To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return, Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return, Recommended: Last year&'s tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents will be accepted., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov. , You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner United Way of King County . Event Logistics: United Way Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025 \(or less than $96,000 for non-single filers\)., For eligibility details, contact United Way at 206-461-3700. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.,

item.title: English Conversation Circle
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 1:30 PM

Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 1 - 2:30pm Practice your English Conversation skills with other adult learners. Practique su inglés. Luyện tập tiếng Anh của quý vị. 练习英语。 練習英語。 英会話の練習をしましょう。 Ku Celceli Ingiriisigaaga. እንግሊዘኛዎንይለማመዱ። እንግሊዘኛኹምተለማመዱ። Afaan Ingilizii kee shaakali. Практикуйте вашу англійську. Практикуйтесь в английском языке. Pratiquez votre anglais. Practique seu inglês. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 4 - Room 1 Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Discuss relevant topics in small groups to gain self-confidence and improve your speaking and listening. This class is for adult English language learners levels 2 and up \(beginner/intermediate\)., The current series run: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, Thanks to our partner Literacy Source ., -----------------------------------------------------------------, Practique su inglés. Hable sobre temas relevantes en grupos pequeños para ganar confianza en sí mismo y mejorar su habla y comprensión auditiva. Esta clase está dirigida a estudiantes adultos de inglés de nivel 2 en adelante \(principiante/intermedio\)., Las clases actuales se imparten del: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Luyện tập tiếng Anh của quý vị. Thảo luận các chủ đề liên quan trong các nhóm nhỏ để có được sự tự tin và cải thiện khả năng nói và nghe của quý vị. Lớp học này dành cho người học tiếng Anh người lớn từ cấp 2 trở lên \(sơ cấp/trung cấp\)., Các khóa học hiện tại diễn ra từ: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, 练习英语。 以小组形式讨论相关话题，增强自信心，提高口语和听力水平。该课程面向 2 级及以上（初级/中级）成人英语学习者。, 当前课程时间： 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, 練習英語。 以小組形式討論相關話題，增強自信心，提高口語和聽力水平。 該課程面向 2 級及以上級別（初級/中級）的成人英語學習者。, 當前課程時間： 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, 英会話の練習をしましょう。 無料の会話グループを見つけ、大人になって英語の学んでいる 方々と一緒にあなたの英語力を磨きましょう。小さいグループ で関連主題を話し合って自信を持たせ、スピーキング力とリス ニング力を向上させてみしょう。, 現在の授業日程： 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Ku Celceli Ingiriisigaaga. Ka wada hadla mawduucyada ku haboon kooxo yaryar si aad u hesho kalsooni aadna u horumariso ku hadalkaaga iyo dhegeysigaaga. Fasalkan waxaa loogu talagalay bartayaasha luqadda Ingiriisiga qaangaarka ah heerarka 2 iyo ka sare \(bilaw/dhexe\)., Fasalada hadda socdaa waxay: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, እንግሊዘኛዎን ይለማመዱ። በራስ መተማመንን ለማግኘት እና ንግግርዎን እና ማዳመጥዎን ለማሻሻል በትናንሽ ቡድኖች ውስጥ ተዛማጅ ርዕሶች ላይ ይወያዩ።. ይህ ክፍል ለአዋቂዎች የእንግሊዝኛ ቋንቋ ተማሪዎች ደረጃ 2 እና ከዚያ በላይ \(ጀማሪ/መካከለኛ\) ነው።, የአሁኑ ክፍሎች የሚካሄዱት ከ፡ 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, እንግሊዘኛኹም ተለማመዱ። ዓርሰ ምትእምማን ንምርካብን ናይ ምዝራብን ምስማዕን ክእለትኩም ንምምሕያሽን ብኣናእሽቶ ጉጅለታት ኮይንኩም ብዛዕባ ኣገደስቲ ኣርእስቲታት ተመያየጡ። እዚ ክፍሊ ንዓበይቲ ተማሃሮ ቋንቋ እንግሊዝ ደረጃ 2ን ልዕሊኡን \(ጀመርቲ/ማእከላይ\) እዩ።, እቶም ሕጂ ዘለዉ ክፍልታት፡ 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Afaan Ingilizii kee shaakali. Ofitti amanamummaa horachuu fi dubbii fi dhaggeeffachuu kee fooyyessuuf garee xixiqqoodhaan mata dureewwan barbaachisoo ta'an irratti mari'adhu. Kutaan kun barattoota afaan Ingiliffaa ga'eessota sadarkaa 2 fi isaa ol \(jalqabaa/giddugaleessa\) ta'aniif., Kutaawwan amma jiran: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Практикуйте вашу англійську. Знайдіть безкоштовні розмовні групи, щоб практикувати свої навички англійської мови разом з іншими дорослими учнями. Обговорюйте актуальні теми в невеликих групах, щоб стати впевненішими в собі та вдосконалити свої навички говоріння й сприйняття мови на слух. Цей клас призначений для дорослих, які вивчають англійську мову на рівні 2 і вище \(початковий/середній\)., Поточні заняття проводяться: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Найдите бесплатные разговорные группы, чтобы прорабатывать свои навыки владения английским с другими взрослыми учащимися. Обсуждайте актуальные темы в малых группах, чтобы обрести уверенность в своих силах и развить умение говорить и воспринимать язык на слух., Текущие занятия проходят: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Pratiquez votre anglais. Échangez sur des sujets qui vous intéressent en petits groupes afin de gagner en confiance et d'améliorer votre expression et votre compéhension orale. Ce cours s'adresse aux adultes de niveau 2 et supérieur \(débutant/intermédiaire\) qui souhaitent apprendre l'anglais., Les cours actuels se déroulent : 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Pratique seu inglês. Discuta tópicos relevantes em pequenos grupos para ganar autoconfiança e melhorar sua fala e audição. Esta aula é para alunos adultos de inglês, nível 2 ou superior \(iniciante/intermediário\)., As aulas atuais decorrem entre: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, -----------------------------------------------------------------, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Event Logistics: Registration before class is not required. Sign in when you arrive., Class size is limited to 20 participants on a first-come, first-served basis. If you arrive more than 15 minutes after class has started, please join us next week. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.

item.title: AI Essentials for Jobs: Boosting Your Job Search Strategy
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 1:30 PM

Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 1:30 - 3pm Discover how to effectively use Artificial Intelligence \(AI\) to enhance your job search and all aspects of your application and interview preparation. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 4 - Room 2 Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Join Employment Specialist Corey Minatani from Evergreen Goodwill&'s Job Training and Education Center \(JTEC\) for an introductory workshop designed to help you leverage artificial intelligence to enhance your job search., Learn how AI can streamline resume building, job matching, interview preparation, and networking, making your job hunt smarter and more efficient. Whether you're new to AI or looking to sharpen your digital tools, this session will provide practical strategies to boost your chances of success in today&'s competitive job market., Thanks to our partner Evergreen Goodwill Job Training & Education. Event Logistics: There will be time after the presentation to ask questions., Find out more about our Employment Workshops . Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.,

Lớp học này dành cho người học tiếng Anh người lớn từ cấp 2 trở lên \(sơ cấp/trung cấp\)., Các khóa học hiện tại diễn ra từ: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, 练习英语。 以小组形式讨论相关话题，增强自信心，提高口语和听力水平。该课程面向 2 级及以上（初级/中级）成人英语学习者。, 当前课程时间： 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, 練習英語。 以小組形式討論相關話題，增強自信心，提高口語和聽力水平。 該課程面向 2 級及以上級別（初級/中級）的成人英語學習者。, 當前課程時間： 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, 英会話の練習をしましょう。 無料の会話グループを見つけ、大人になって英語の学んでいる 方々と一緒にあなたの英語力を磨きましょう。小さいグループ で関連主題を話し合って自信を持たせ、スピーキング力とリス ニング力を向上させてみしょう。, 現在の授業日程： 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Ku Celceli Ingiriisigaaga. Ka wada hadla mawduucyada ku haboon kooxo yaryar si aad u hesho kalsooni aadna u horumariso ku hadalkaaga iyo dhegeysigaaga. Fasalkan waxaa loogu talagalay bartayaasha luqadda Ingiriisiga qaangaarka ah heerarka 2 iyo ka sare \(bilaw/dhexe\)., Fasalada hadda socdaa waxay: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, እንግሊዘኛዎን ይለማመዱ። በራስ መተማመንን ለማግኘት እና ንግግርዎን እና ማዳመጥዎን ለማሻሻል በትናንሽ ቡድኖች ውስጥ ተዛማጅ ርዕሶች ላይ ይወያዩ።. ይህ ክፍል ለአዋቂዎች የእንግሊዝኛ ቋንቋ ተማሪዎች ደረጃ 2 እና ከዚያ በላይ \(ጀማሪ/መካከለኛ\) ነው።, የአሁኑ ክፍሎች የሚካሄዱት ከ፡ 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, እንግሊዘኛኹም ተለማመዱ። ዓርሰ ምትእምማን ንምርካብን ናይ ምዝራብን ምስማዕን ክእለትኩም ንምምሕያሽን ብኣናእሽቶ ጉጅለታት ኮይንኩም ብዛዕባ ኣገደስቲ ኣርእስቲታት ተመያየጡ። እዚ ክፍሊ ንዓበይቲ ተማሃሮ ቋንቋ እንግሊዝ ደረጃ 2ን ልዕሊኡን \(ጀመርቲ/ማእከላይ\) እዩ።, እቶም ሕጂ ዘለዉ ክፍልታት፡ 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Afaan Ingilizii kee shaakali. Ofitti amanamummaa horachuu fi dubbii fi dhaggeeffachuu kee fooyyessuuf garee xixiqqoodhaan mata dureewwan barbaachisoo ta'an irratti mari'adhu. Kutaan kun barattoota afaan Ingiliffaa ga'eessota sadarkaa 2 fi isaa ol \(jalqabaa/giddugaleessa\) ta'aniif., Kutaawwan amma jiran: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Практикуйте вашу англійську. Знайдіть безкоштовні розмовні групи, щоб практикувати свої навички англійської мови разом з іншими дорослими учнями. Обговорюйте актуальні теми в невеликих групах, щоб стати впевненішими в собі та вдосконалити свої навички говоріння й сприйняття мови на слух. Цей клас призначений для дорослих, які вивчають англійську мову на рівні 2 і вище \(початковий/середній\)., Поточні заняття проводяться: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Найдите бесплатные разговорные группы, чтобы прорабатывать свои навыки владения английским с другими взрослыми учащимися. Обсуждайте актуальные темы в малых группах, чтобы обрести уверенность в своих силах и развить умение говорить и воспринимать язык на слух., Текущие занятия проходят: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Pratiquez votre anglais. Échangez sur des sujets qui vous intéressent en petits groupes afin de gagner en confiance et d'améliorer votre expression et votre compéhension orale. Ce cours s'adresse aux adultes de niveau 2 et supérieur \(débutant/intermédiaire\) qui souhaitent apprendre l'anglais., Les cours actuels se déroulent : 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Pratique seu inglês. Discuta tópicos relevantes em pequenos grupos para ganar autoconfiança e melhorar sua fala e audição. Esta aula é para alunos adultos de inglês, nível 2 ou superior \(iniciante/intermediário\)., As aulas atuais decorrem entre: 3/31/2026 - 6/9/2026, -----------------------------------------------------------------, -----------------------------------------------------------------, -----------------------------------------------------------------, Event Logistics: Registration before class is not required. Sign in when you arrive., Class size is limited to 20 participants on a first-come, first-served basis. If you arrive more than 15 minutes after class has started, please join us next week. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. item.title: AI Essentials for Jobs: Boosting Your Job Search Strategy
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 1:30 PM

Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 1:30 - 3pm Discover how to effectively use Artificial Intelligence \(AI\) to enhance your job search and all aspects of your application and interview preparation. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 4 - Room 2 Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Join Employment Specialist Corey Minatani from Evergreen Goodwill&'s Job Training and Education Center \(JTEC\) for an introductory workshop designed to help you leverage artificial intelligence to enhance your job search., Learn how AI can streamline resume building, job matching, interview preparation, and networking, making your job hunt smarter and more efficient. Whether you're new to AI or looking to sharpen your digital tools, this session will provide practical strategies to boost your chances of success in today&'s competitive job market., Thanks to our partner Evergreen Goodwill Job Training & Education. Event Logistics: There will be time after the presentation to ask questions., Find out more about our Employment Workshops . item.description:

item.title: Homework Help
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 3:30 - 7:30pm Homework Help volunteers will be at this branch to assist K-12 students with school assignments during the school year. Location Link: Northgate Branch Room Location: Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: If you are working on a tough homework assignment or have questions about a lesson, come to the Library for Homework Help. We have volunteers who can help with math, English, social studies and science., Students without homework can read and play learning enrichment games with trained tutors., Snacks are provided at every Homework Help: Thanks to the donors of The Seattle Public Library Foundation for sponsoring this event. Event Logistics: , Registration is not required but these programs are very popular and the room capacity can fill up quickly., While Homework Help is available to students from kindergarten through 12th grade, volunteers are not responsible for children, who should not be left unsupervised., Find out more about Homework Help. item.title: Slow Flow Community Yoga
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 4:30 PM

Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 4:30 - 5:30pm Join us for all-levels slow flow community yoga. Location Link: Greenwood Branch Room Location: Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Yoga instructors Lucia Hunt and Emily Dalgo provide slow flow yoga classes with trauma-informed cueing to make the classes welcoming, accessible, and affirming of all bodies and identities., There will be no expectation of certainyogaexperience to attend classes. Modifications will be offered throughout classes, encouraging people to listen to what feels good in their body and make adjustments as they need and want to. Event Logistics: Yoga mats can be provided at each session., Free parking is available in the Greenwood Branch parking garage and on nearby streets., Patrons joining this class will be asked to complete a liability waiver by the instructors. item.title: Power Connect: Bombo
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 4:30 PM

Location: Bombo Italian Kitchen, 1600 9th Ave, Seattle, WA

Description Power Connect at Bombo Italian Date: Tuesday, March 31, 2026 Time: 4:30 PM Location: Bombo Italian Presented by: U.S. Bank Tickets: $25 for members and $35 for guests Join GSBA for a new Power Connect at Bombo Italian on Tuesday, March 31 at 4:30 PM. Power Connect is our signature networking series-an easygoing space to meet fellow LGBTQ\+ and allied professionals, reconnect with familiar faces, and build relationships that support your business and our community. Come as you are, bring a colleague, and expect the standard Power Connect vibes: warm welcomes, real conversation, and connections that keep going after the event. What to Expect A welcoming, relationship-first environment Space for casual conversation and meaningful introductions A great chance to meet GSBA members, partners, and friends An easy way to plug into the GSBA community Accessibility If you have access needs or accommodation requests, please reach out to GSBA in advance and we'll do our best to support you: membership@thegsb.org Thank You to Our Presenting Sponsor Power Connect: Bombo is presented by U.S. Bank, supporting LGBTQ\+ and allied businesses and helping strengthen connections across our region.

What to Expect A welcoming, relationship-first environment Space for casual conversation and meaningful introductions A great chance to meet GSBA members, partners, and friends An easy way to plug into the GSBA community Accessibility If you have access needs or accommodation requests, please reach out to GSBA in advance and we'll do our best to support you: membership@thegsb.org Thank You to Our Presenting Sponsor Power Connect: Bombo is presented by U.S. Bank, supporting LGBTQ\+ and allied businesses and helping strengthen connections across our region.

Event details

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

item.title: Moomins' Sea Adventures & Tove and the Sea
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

Event details

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

item.title: Moomins' Sea Adventures & Tove and the Sea
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

item.title: Small Frye: Storytelling + Art
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 10:15 AM

Location: Frye Art Museum, 704 Terry Ave, Seattle, WA

Registration will open approximately a month before the event. Enjoy a lively retelling of a beloved children’s book, then create art with loved ones using materials designed to spark tactile play and wonder. The hands-on art activity, inspired by "Priscilla Dobler Dzul: Water Carries the Stories of Our Stars," is designed to enhance the skills and developmental strengths of pre-K children in collaboration with their caregivers. This Small Frye session features the picture book Goldilocks and the Three Dinosaurs by Mo Willems. Small Frye is free, but registration is required. Space is limited—sign up today! --- https://fryemuseum.org/calendar/event/small-frye-april-2026

item.title: Boren Banner Series: Camille Trautman
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Frye Art Museum, 704 Terry Ave, Seattle, WA

Every photograph is a reminder that the act of framing is never neutral. In Camille Trautman’s first solo museum exhibition in their hometown, the Seattle-born Duwamish artist uses photography and video to challenge colonial narratives and counter Indigenous erasure. The exhibition presents selections from their ongoing series The North American LCD—spectral self-portraits staged in varied natural landscapes. Rooted in Trautman’s process of coming out as transgender, the images show the artist’s body partially obscured by large LCD screens. The series examines how both landscape photography and digital media can shape or distort cultural identity, often by promoting or denying visibility.

Location: Frye Art Museum, 704 Terry Ave, Seattle, WA

Every photograph is a reminder that the act of framing is never neutral. In Camille Trautman’s first solo museum exhibition in their hometown, the Seattle-born Duwamish artist uses photography and video to challenge colonial narratives and counter Indigenous erasure. The exhibition presents selections from their ongoing series The North American LCD—spectral self-portraits staged in varied natural landscapes. Rooted in Trautman’s process of coming out as transgender, the images show the artist’s body partially obscured by large LCD screens. The series examines how both landscape photography and digital media can shape or distort cultural identity, often by promoting or denying visibility.

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Book-It Repertory Theatre, 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, WA

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session\\\*\*\* Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate \(paper, t-shirts, etc\) - Water based ink Vera \*does not\* allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. \*\*FAQ\*\* 1. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

Event details

item.title: Moomins' Sea Adventures & Tove and the Sea
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Figure/Ground: New Criteria
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Figure/Ground: New Criteria is inspired by cultural historian Sarah Lewis’s concept of “groundwork aesthetics,” exploring figure-ground relationships and their connection to social tension. It builds on Lewis’s work, which—set against the expansion of Stand Your Ground laws across the United States since 2005, laws that broadly define rights to use lethal self-defense against perceived threats—describes a range of artistic interventions that reimagine “the literal and figurative meaning of ground.” Primarily featuring works from the Henry collection created in the twenty-first century, Figure/Ground reflects a period in which hard-won civil rights and claims to self-determination have been eroded across the US, disproportionately affecting Black, Brown, LGBTQ\+, and other marginalized communities.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Eric-Paul Riege at Burke Artist Studio
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

To celebrate the opening weekend of Eric-Paul Riege: ojo|-|ólǫ́, visit the artist offsite at the Burke Artist Studio, located in the Northwest Native Art Gallery at the Burke Museum, just a few blocks from the Henry. As part of Free First Thursday at the Burke, visitors will have the chance to watch the artist at work and speak with Riege about his process. ojo|-|ólǫ́ is based on Eric-Paul Riege's material research and engagement with the Navajo collections held by the Burke Museum, as well as the Brown University's Haffenreffer Museum of Anthropology. The resulting new body of work is informed by ancestral knowledge and traditions contained within Indigenous objects, and which aims, in part, to redress practices of cultural dispossession by museums and other institutions. Bio Eric-Paul Riege \(Diné\) is a weaver and fiber artist working in collage, durational performance, installation, woven sculpture, and wearable art. Using weaving as both means and metaphor to tell hybrid tales that interlace stories from Diné spirituality with his own interpretations and cosmology, he understands his artworks as animate and mobile. His practice pays homage and links him to generations of weavers in his family who aid him in generating spaces of sanctuary. Riege's recent solo exhibitions include iiZiiT \[3\]: RIEGE Jewelry \+ Supply at Canal Projects, New York \(2025\), Hammer Projects: Eric Paul Riege at the Hammer Museum, Los Angeles \(2022-2023\), and Hól ́ǫ-it xistz at the Institute of Contemporary Art Miami \(2019\). His recent group exhibitions include the 24th Biennale of Sydney in Australia \(2024\), Indian Theater: Native Performance, Art, and Self-Determination \(2023\), Prospect.5 Triennial in New Orleans \(2022\), and the Toronto Biennial of Art \(2022\). He holds a BFA in Art Studio and Ecology from the University of New Mexico in Albuquerque. His work is collected by Forge Project and ICA Miami, among others. He is represented by Bockley Gallery \(MN\) and STARS Gallery \(CA\). Riege is a member of the Charcoal Streaked Division of the Red Running Into the Water clan. He was born and is based in Na'nízhoozhí \[Gallup, New Mexico\].

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Call your friends and bring the family, admission is FREE to everyone at the Seattle Art Museum on the first Thursday of every month. In addition to our regular hours, Seattle Art Museum will be open for Free First Thursday evenings from 5-8 pm to provide more opportunities to visit the galleries for free! Evening hours will feature extra activities like live music, drop-in art making, and in-gallery experiences for visitors. 11:30 am, 1:30 pm, 6 pm | Public Tour: Highlights from SAM's Collection These 60-minute tours meet at the top of the 3rd floor escalators. 5:30-6:30 pm | SAM Gallery Artist Talk SAM Gallery Free First Thursday Evenings Presented by
