item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Kameelah Janan Rasheed: we leak, we exceed
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Kameelah Janan Rasheed \(b. 1985, East Palo Alto, CA; lives and works in Brooklyn, NY\) is a self-described “learner” who creates multi-media works that explore the poetics-pleasures-politics of Black knowledge production, information technologies, \[un\]learning, and belief formation. She often integrates found images and words alongside her own writing to create collaged wall installations and experimental video works. Across her work, Rasheed investigates the revision and life cycles of text, embracing expanded and embodied forms of writing and reading. we leak, we exceed will activate the unique volume and multiple vantage points of the Henry’s double-height gallery, drawing together threads from physics, Black critical thought, and information theory to create an immersive environment that interrogates the spatial and social implications of compression. A common process used in data storage, spatial organization, and information systems, compression abbreviates and collapses complex ideas into more simplified forms. Rasheed questions the way compression comes at the cost of nuance and creates unrecoverable losses. She draws parallels between the compression of information and the containment of people, both physically and through the structuring and defining of identities. Through a network of video, sound, and architectural mark-making, Rasheed proposes alternatively what she calls “an embrace of Black excess and expansion” as a liberatory practice.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: Figure/Ground: New Criteria
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Figure/Ground: New Criteria is inspired by cultural historian Sarah Lewis’s concept of “groundwork aesthetics,” exploring figure-ground relationships and their connection to social tension. It builds on Lewis’s work, which—set against the expansion of Stand Your Ground laws across the United States since 2005, laws that broadly define rights to use lethal self-defense against perceived threats—describes a range of artistic interventions that reimagine “the literal and figurative meaning of ground.” Primarily featuring works from the Henry collection created in the twenty-first century, Figure/Ground reflects a period in which hard-won civil rights and claims to self-determination have been eroded across the US, disproportionately affecting Black, Brown, LGBTQ\+, and other marginalized communities.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: Kameelah Janan Rasheed: we leak, we exceed
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Henry Art Gallery, 15th Ave NE & 41st St, Seattle, WA

Kameelah Janan Rasheed \(b. 1985, East Palo Alto, CA; lives and works in Brooklyn, NY\) is a self-described “learner” who creates multi-media works that explore the poetics-pleasures-politics of Black knowledge production, information technologies, \[un\]learning, and belief formation. She often integrates found images and words alongside her own writing to create collaged wall installations and experimental video works. Across her work, Rasheed investigates the revision and life cycles of text, embracing expanded and embodied forms of writing and reading. we leak, we exceed will activate the unique volume and multiple vantage points of the Henry’s double-height gallery, drawing together threads from physics, Black critical thought, and information theory to create an immersive environment that interrogates the spatial and social implications of compression. A common process used in data storage, spatial organization, and information systems, compression abbreviates and collapses complex ideas into more simplified forms. Rasheed questions the way compression comes at the cost of nuance and creates unrecoverable losses. She draws parallels between the compression of information and the containment of people, both physically and through the structuring and defining of identities. Through a network of video, sound, and architectural mark-making, Rasheed proposes alternatively what she calls “an embrace of Black excess and expansion” as a liberatory practice.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

item.title: Boren Banner Series: Camille Trautman
item.description:
Sunday, April 5, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Frye Art Museum, 704 Terry Ave, Seattle, WA

Every photograph is a reminder that the act of framing is never neutral. In Camille Trautman’s first solo museum exhibition in their hometown, the Seattle-born Duwamish artist uses photography and video to challenge colonial narratives and counter Indigenous erasure. The exhibition presents selections from their ongoing series The North American LCD—spectral self-portraits staged in varied natural landscapes. Rooted in Trautman’s process of coming out as transgender, the images show the artist’s body partially obscured by large LCD screens. The series examines how both landscape photography and digital media can shape or distort cultural identity, often by promoting or denying visibility.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Spy Masters Academy
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Suncatcher Forest School, 18701 se 60th st, Issaquah, WA

Class Experience Calling all young secret agents! This time kids are welcome to join action-packed Outdoor Spy Camp, where kids will train to become top-notch spies through thrilling challenges, daring missions, and teamwork! Obstacle Courses & Ninja Training – Agents-in-training will test their agility, speed, and stealth as they navigate exciting ninja courses, crawl through the forest and learn to use nature objects to complete the challenge. Scavenger Hunts & Riddle Solving – Spies must sharpen their detective skills by decoding secret messages, solving riddles, and uncovering hidden clues to complete their top-secret missions. Nerf Battles & Tactical Challenges – Gear up for friendly Nerf wars, where teamwork and strategy are key to outsmarting the opposition. Agents will also practice their precision and accuracy with target practice and stealth maneuvers. Escape Room-Style Puzzles – Secret agents will work together to crack codes, unlock treasure chests, and solve mind-bending puzzles before time runs out! Outdoor Adventure & Camouflage Training – Spies will blend into their surroundings, practice silent movement, and use nature to their advantage while completing their missions. This high-energy camp is perfect for kids who love adventure, problem-solving, and action-packed fun in the great outdoors! Are you ready to accept the mission? - follow the ticket link to book the camp!

item.title: Spy Masters Academy
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Suncatcher Forest School, 18701 se 60th st, Issaquah, WA

Class Experience Calling all young secret agents! This time kids are welcome to join action-packed Outdoor Spy Camp, where kids will train to become top-notch spies through thrilling challenges, daring missions, and teamwork! Obstacle Courses & Ninja Training – Agents-in-training will test their agility, speed, and stealth as they navigate exciting ninja courses, crawl through the forest and learn to use nature objects to complete the challenge. Scavenger Hunts & Riddle Solving – Spies must sharpen their detective skills by decoding secret messages, solving riddles, and uncovering hidden clues to complete their top-secret missions. Nerf Battles & Tactical Challenges – Gear up for friendly Nerf wars, where teamwork and strategy are key to outsmarting the opposition. Agents will also practice their precision and accuracy with target practice and stealth maneuvers. Escape Room-Style Puzzles – Secret agents will work together to crack codes, unlock treasure chests, and solve mind-bending puzzles before time runs out! Outdoor Adventure & Camouflage Training – Spies will blend into their surroundings, practice silent movement, and use nature to their advantage while completing their missions. This high-energy camp is perfect for kids who love adventure, problem-solving, and action-packed fun in the great outdoors! Are you ready to accept the mission? - follow the ticket link to book the camp!

From SEAtoday - Learning & Education

item.title: March Madness Watch Party at Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Five Iron Golf - Seattle Capitol Hill, 1525 11th Ave Suite 100, Seattle, WA

Five Iron Golf is your College Basketball HQ this March! Gather your crew and book 1–2 simulator bays for up to 12 people \(6 people per bay\) for a bracket-busting watch party experience. Enjoy all-day TV coverage of college basketball alongside cutting-edge golf and multisport simulators, a locally-inspired menu, and custom beverage packages. Book your watch party for March 19 or 20 and you'll automatically be entered to win a 20-person happy hour with drinks and appetizers — a $1,000 value! Group packages can include reserved event space, team-specific décor, a dedicated staff member, full-screen and/or picture-in-picture viewing, a customizable menu, beverage packages, free club rentals, multisport simulators, and table games. Select the "Small Event" option at booking to ensure simulator bays are placed side-by-side. Availability of select features varies by location. Promotions: Happy Hour – "It's 5i o'Clock Somewhere" \(Monday–Friday, 3–6 PM\) Enjoy $5 bottomless boneless wings, $10 bottomless bone-in wings, $5 select beers/cans/cocktails, $5 dips \(guacamole, hot honey ricotta, hummus\), half-off pizzas, and $5 featured shots. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required. 5i After Dark – "Swing In, For Less"  \(Sunday–Thursday 9 PM to close | Friday–Saturday 10 PM to close\) Features $29/hr simulator rentals plus the same great food and drink specials as Happy Hour, with wings priced per dozen rather than bottomless. Dine-in only at participating locations; simulator rental may be required.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Capitol Hill & First Hill Business Safety Summit
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Stoup Brewing, 1158 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Description You are invited to join fellow Capitol Hill and First Hill businesses for a Safety Summit at the Silver Cloud Hotel \(1100 Broadway\) on Mon, Apr 6 from 3:00 to 5:00 p.m. This gathering is designed specifically for businesses operating at the street level. This is a great opportunity to meet the new East Precinct Captain, hear about helpful programs from the City, and connect with neighboring businesses facing many of the same challenges. Our goal is to work together to support and strengthen what makes the business districts of Capitol Hill and First Hill so unique and special. This event is brought to you by the 15th Avenue Business Improvement Area, Broadway Business Improvement Area, First Hill Improvement Association, and GSBA: Capitol Hill, with additional support from 4N & Associates and Silver Cloud Hotel. Meet Our New East Precinct Captain Captain James Britt, SPD Community Assisted Response & Engagement \(CARE\) Chief Amy Barden, CARE Learn About Community Assisted Response & Engagement \(CARE\) Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design \(CPTED\) Back to Business Program: Storefront Repair Fund and Storefront Security Fund Share Bring your safety challenges, concerns and questions Program 3pm Doors open 3:30pm Speaking program starts followed by social Please RSVP Space is limited. Free Parking! Light food provided. Please bring cash for no host bar. Pre-event Survey Help shape the conversation at the Safety Summit by sharing any safety challenges, concerns, or questions you would like addressed. Please submit responses by Friday, April 3 so we can share common themes with presenters in advance. If you cannot attend, we still welcome your input and will share an event recap afterward.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Building a More Welcoming Washington: Seattle's FIFA World Cup Inclusion Training
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: GSBA- Washington State&'s LGBTQ+ Chamber of Commerce, 400 E Pine St Ste 322, Seattle, WA

Description Inclusion Training Initiative: Building a More Welcoming Washington As Washington prepares to welcome the world for FIFA World Cup 2026, local businesses will be on the front lines of hospitality-serving visitors, supporting staff, and representing the values of our region every day. GSBA is partnering with SeattleFWC26 to offer Inclusion Training workshops designed to help small businesses and organizations feel prepared, confident, and welcoming. These sessions provide practical, real-world guidance on allyship, gender inclusion, and LGBTQ\+ cultural competency, with a focus on everyday customer and workplace interactions. About the Inclusion Training Initiative As part of SeattleFWC26's commitment to ensuring every fan, visitor, and resident feels welcome, the Inclusion Training Initiative-delivered in partnership with GSBA-equips local businesses and organizations with tools to create authentically inclusive environments for LGBTQ\+ customers, employees, and communities. About River Teslar River \(she/her\) is a trans woman and nonprofit professional with four years of experience at GSBA developing and facilitating LGBTQ\+ Inclusion Trainings through a social justice- and trauma-informed lens. Drawing from lived experience and community-based work, she creates learning spaces grounded in empathy, curiosity, and practical application, where participants feel supported engaging at any level. Key Activities ⚽ Four Inclusion Workshops: Hosted by GSBA and tailored for small businesses, nonprofits, and frontline teams. ⚽ Interactive Learning: Sessions focus on allyship, gender inclusion, and LGBTQ\+ cultural competency in the workplace, with real-world scenarios and discussion. ⚽ Accessible Participation: Two in-person workshops hosted at GSBA's Capitol Hill office \(or other community venues\) and two virtual workshops open to participants statewide. ⚽ Practical Tools: Participants receive actionable guidance and resources to help strengthen inclusive policies, communications, and customer experiences. Outcomes & Benefits ⚽ More Inclusive Businesses: Participating organizations gain the knowledge to create spaces that welcome all genders, identities, and relationships. ⚽ Community Visibility: Strengthens Washington's reputation as a leader in inclusive tourism and community hospitality ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026. ⚽ Economic Impact: Inclusive environments encourage repeat visitation, word-of-mouth referrals, and stronger customer loyalty. ⚽ Sustained Legacy: Builds long-term capacity for inclusion that extends beyond the tournament, leaving a lasting mark on Washington's business and tourism ecosystem. Workshop Schedule Virtual Workshops ⚽ Thursday, January 15 - 10:00 AM ⚽ Wednesday, March 4 - 3:00 PM In-Person Workshops ⚽ Monday, February 9 - 10:00 AM ⚽ Monday, April 6 - 3:00 PM Learn more about the initiative: SeattleFWC26 Inclusion Training Initiative

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Cooking from Scratch: Skills, Techniques & Recipes
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Learn how to cook like a pro! Build your confidence in the kitchen while mastering essential cooking methods and techniques.  Whether you’re just starting out or already love to cook, this fun, hands-on class will help you master the building blocks of great cooking. Learn how to handle a knife with confidence, prepare proteins, and turn fresh vegetables and hearty starches into flavorful dishes. Explore the secrets behind rich stocks and silky sauces, as well as the magic of hydrocolloids \(for thickening and gelling\).  Discover how to balance flavors with finesse, work efficiently with smart kitchen mechanics, and keep things safe and clean with proper sanitation. Come sharpen your skills, boost your confidence, and enjoy the satisfaction of creating meals that truly shine. Learning Objectives Cooking methods and skills Knife skills  Working with proteins, vegetables and starches Preparing stocks and sauces, and using hydrocolloids Balancing flavors Kitchen mechanics, sanitation and safety  Who Might Enjoy This Class? Home cooks who would like to cook better, diversify what they make, get better results, and be more efficient with their time in the kitchen  Anyone ready to learn cooking fundamentals and improve their skills  Competent cooks and professionals wanting to fill in gaps in their skills and learn from a pro

item.title: Raised By TV Events Presents: *ALL AGES* The Office Trivia Night
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Clock-Out Lounge, 4864 Beacon Ave S, Seattle, WA

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

item.title: The Garden w/ Ghost Mountain
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Showbox SoDo, 1700 1st Ave. South, Seattle, WA

THE GARDEN IS A CONSTANTLY EVOLVING BAND ORIGINALLY FROM ORANGE COUNTY, CALIFORNIA. ESTABLISHED IN 2011 BY WYATT AND FLETCHER SHEARS. THEY HAVE BEEN TOURING SINCE 2012 AND HAVE PLAYED IN DIFFERENT REGIONS INCLUDING THE UNITED KINGDOM, EUROPE, UNITED STATES, ASIA, AUSTRALIA, MEXICO & CANADA, THEY CREATED THE TERM, "VADA VADA" IN 2011 TO REPRESENT THEIR MUSIC AND OTHER CREATIONS. VADA VADA IS A TERM THAT REPRESENTS TOTAL FREEDOM OF EXPRESSION WITHOUT BOUNDARIES OR GUIDELINES OF ANY SORT. THEY HAVE BECOME KNOWN FOR THEIR ENERGETIC AND OFF THE WALL PERFORMANCES AROUND THE WORLD, AND ARE PROLIFICALLY TOURING, RELEASING MUSIC AND OTHER CONTENT.

item.title: Geeks Who Drink Trivia at Stone's Throw Coffee & Market
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Stone's Throw Coffee & Market, 1218 N 45th St, Seattle, WA

Geeks Who Drink Trivia is an authentic, homegrown pub quiz, where useless knowledge means everything. Our general knowledge quizzes cover everything from literature to lit memes, from pop culture to the history of PopTarts—crafted in creative, multimedia rounds that’ll keep you thinking and laughing all night. Bring your cleverest friends \(or your dumbest, if you want to look extra smart\), or play solo and meet some fresh faces. Free & fun to play, all you need is your phone! Join us at Stone's Throw Coffee & Market every Monday, where the fun starts at 7pm. To learn more, visit geekswhodrink.com.

item.title: Wife of Headless Man Investigates Her Own Disappearance
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Annex Theatre, 1100 E Pike St, Seattle, WA

Written by Yussef El Guindi Directed by Lucien Oberleitner A reporter experiences a chunk of missing time after interviewing a tech billionaire who hasn’t taken kindly to her negative articles about him. Was she drugged during the meal? How did she end up in a hotel room after meeting him in a restaurant? And if she was drugged, is that why, when she returns home, her husband appears as fully alive and functioning and headless?

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Moomins' Sea Adventures & Tove and the Sea
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

item.title: Earth Month Event | Mindfulness Walk
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 11 AM

Celebrate Earth Month with a Mindfulness Walk in the Park! Taking inspiration from the Japanese idea of shinrin-yoku \(forest bathin g\), we will be hosting a series of mindfulness walks in Pioneer Park. Mindfulness walking in the forest has shown to have a myriad of benefits from improving heart health to reducing stress \(https://www.npr.org/2023/08/22 /1195337204/a-guide-to-forest-bathing\). Forest Steward Marilyn Cornwe ll \(https://www.soulscapes.us/\) will be leading an hour-long guided walk t hrough the park. Please join us if your curious about mindfulness, want to learn more about and experience healing powers of being in nature, and/or if you want to deepen your connection with Pioneer Park and others. When: Tuesday, April 4th, 11am-12pm Where: Pioneer Park NW, Meet at the corner of Island Crest Way and SE 68th St. Follow VOLUNTEER EVENT Signs What to bring: Dress for the weather in clothes and shoes you will comfo rtable walking and standing in, as well as water.

item.title: Digital Humanities: Intersections and Challenges
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Advancement & Alumni Building, Stuart T. Rolfe Room, Seattle, WA

What Is Digital Humanities—and Why Does It Matter Now? Digital humanities is an interdisciplinary field that combines humanistic inquiry with digital tools to transform how we study culture, language, history, and society. From digital archives and multilingual publishing to data-driven cultural analysis, the field is reshaping research, teaching, and public scholarship worldwide. The Roundglass India Center & Technology Ethics Initiative invite students and faculty to explore this rapidly evolving space through a special lecture by Professor Nirmala Menon, an internationally recognized leader in digital humanities. Professor Menon is Chair Professor at the School of Humanities and Social Sciences at the Indian Institute of Technology Indore, where she leads the Digital Humanities and Publishing Research Group and is Chair of the JPN National Centre of Excellence in the Humanities. She also serves as an Affiliate Research Professor at the University of Oxford and is currently a Fulbright Fellow at Northeastern University \(2025–26\). Her scholarship spans postcolonial studies, digital humanities, and multilingual knowledge systems, with a sustained focus on decolonizing global knowledge infrastructures. Dr. Menon's Talk: Digital Knowledge Infrastructures and Digital Humanities Projects: Intersections and Challenges In this timely talk, Professor Menon examines the uneven and evolving trajectory of digital humanities \(DH\) in India and the broader research ecosystem. She explores the skepticism and resistance the field sometimes encounters—including concerns about distancing from core humanities questions or borrowing the rhetoric of STEM for legitimacy—while making the case that DH is far more than its critiques. Professor Menon argues that humanities scholars must actively harness, interrogate, and shape digital technologies to ensure that enduring humanistic questions remain central to technological development and deployment. Drawing on major initiatives—including Project KSHIP at IIT Indore, the DST-sponsored SINDHU project, the Electronic Literature from India anthology, and the work of Open Access India—she highlights efforts to decolonize knowledge systems, expand multilingual scholarship, and make research more accessible and equitable. The talk will also address the continuing structural challenges facing humanities research infrastructures and invite conversation about the future of digital scholarship in global and Indian contexts. Students, faculty, and researchers interested in digital scholarship, global humanities, publishing, technology and society, and South Asia are especially encouraged to attend.

item.title: 1st
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: SCERA, 625 S. 4th Street, Renton, WA

MAXIMIZE YOUR MEMBERSHIP Congratulations, you have joined the Chamber of Commerce! Now what? Here is where you learn to use the SUPER 6 Tools, when you log in to your MIC \(Member Information Center\). Maximize Your Membership 101 workshop is designed to educate our valued members on the benefits of using your Chamber Master access. We will also cover networking opportunities and advocacy that are available to you as a member of the Renton Chamber of Commerce. This training session will equip you with the ability to navigate and utilize your MIC Tools to effectively promote your business reaching over 10k potential customers. Posting Jobs, Events, Member 2 Member and Hot Deals, and more, will be shown. MYM101 is an essential to for new members onboarding and current members who need a refresher course, who want MAXimize Your Membership! Our membership is similar to a gym... the more you use it, the better the results! We also welcome businesses who are interested in chamber membership to join us! FREE!

item.title: City Council Regular Hybrid Meeting
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 5 PM

January 29, 20206 - Notice is hereby given that the Mercer Island City Council will hold a public hearing at its Hybrid Meeting on Tuesd ay, March 3, 2026, at approximately 5pm, to receive comments on Ordina nce No. 26-02, renewing interim regulations in MICC 19.16.010 related to emergency shelters and housing, transitional housing, and permanent supportive housing in response to E2SHB 1220. The public hearing will b e held in person and using Zoom. The public will have the opportunity to c omment during the public hearing by either attending in person, calling in, or logging onto the meeting via Zoom. Written comments may be submitted to the City of Mercer Island by e-mail to council@mercerisland.gov until such time that the public hearing is adjourned. Detailed instruction s on how to comment live during the public hearing will be available onlin e on or before February 25, 2026, at: https://www.mercerisland.gov/citycouncil. Andrea Larson, CMC City Clerk Notice: City Council Calendar - January 29, 2026 City Council Agenda Distribution List - January 29, 2026 Website Public Notice - January 29, 2026 Mercer Island Reporter - January 28, 2026

item.title: Entrepreneurial Mash Up
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Columbia Tower Club, 701 5th Ave, 75th Flr, Seattle, WA

Description Entrepreneurial Mash Up The city's doers, dreamers, and makers in one place. Date: Tuesday, April 7 Time: 5:30-7:30 PM Location: Columbia Tower Club Price: $35 Come meet the communities shaping Seattle's entrepreneurial soul. Columbia Tower Club, GSBA, and Entrepreneurs' Organization are coming together to host an evening built for connection, curiosity, and shared momentum. This is your chance to learn what each organization brings to the region's business landscape and meet the people who make those missions real. Expect conversations that open doors, introductions that expand your world, and a room filled with good energy and big ideas. Show up ready to connect. Leave with a wider circle and a stronger network. This event is for active GSBA members only - you must be logged in to your InfoHub to purchase tickets. Due to extremely limited capacity, each organization has access to 35 tickets. About the Hosts About GSBA GSBA is Washington State's LGBTQ\+ and allied chamber of commerce. GSBA invests in economic vitality, business development, and inclusive leadership through programming, advocacy, and the GSBA Scholarship & Education Fund. About EO Seattle EO Seattle is the local chapter of the Entrepreneurs' Organization, a global network of founders and business owners committed to learning, growth, and community. Members connect through peer learning, events, and leadership development opportunities. About Columbia Tower Club Located at the top of Columbia Center, Columbia Tower Club is Seattle's premier private business and social club. The Club convenes leaders from across industries for dining, professional connection, and memorable experiences.

item.title: Type Design Basics
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview So you want to design a typeface? Learn how to hand-draw letters and digitize them to create your own unique functional font! In this practical workshop, students will create an original text typeface based on traditional European calligraphy. Whether you want to develop your typography skills, improve your lettering practice, or become a type designer, learning the fundamentals of type design can bring your skills to the next level. Explore both analog media \(pen on paper\) and digital media \(computer\). Learn how to use a calligraphy pen to develop an understanding of the underlying structure of the Latin alphabet. Then you can work on improving your drawings digitally, and create a typeface using Glyphs \(or the font editor of your choice\).   Class time is dedicated to working on your project with individualized feedback, supplemented by lectures, technical demonstrations, and group critique. By the end of the class, you will have a working text typeface of your own creation, as well as a sketch-to-screen workflow that you can use for future font creation. Students will be encouraged to spend up to 10 hours on homework outside of class time. Designing a typeface is a laborious process, but time flies when experimenting, drawing, and editing! This class is offered in partnership with the amazing Seattle Creative Academy \(but this class is not part of their program and is not for credit/degree\)! See also: Experimental Lettering and other Graphic Design or art classes on our website. Learning Objectives Learn the basics of calligraphy Understand the structure and patterning of the Latin alphabet Create your own original letterforms Digitize and refine your original design Learn how to optimize a typeface for text Become familiar with a sketch-to-screen digital type design workflow Who Might Enjoy This Class? Anyone who wants to develop their own custom font Graphic designers, illustrators, lettering artists, type designers -- anyone with related experience who would like to hone their type design skills Architects, engineers, artists, crafters, writers, book lovers -- anyone interested in the form of letters who would enjoy creating and using their own handmade font No prerequisites, but basic graphic design/typography knowledge is recommended

item.title: Currents & ERRA
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: The Showbox, 1426 1st Ave., Seattle, WA

Showbox Presents The Silence Follows Tour 2026 CURRENTS & ERRA with Caskets and Aviana TUE, 7 APR 2026 at 06:30PM PDT Ages: All Ages to Enter, 21 & Over to Drink Doors Open: 06:00PM OnSale: Fri, 19 Dec 2025 at 10:00AM PST Announcement: Tue, 16 Dec 2025 at 07:00AM PST Currents Currents never compromise neither creatively, nor philosophically.As if controlling chaos by hand, the Connecticut quintet—Brian Wille \[vocals\], Chris Wiseman \[guitar\], RyanCastaldi \[guitar\], Matt Young \[drums\], and Christian Pulgarin \[bass\]—balance an inclination for invention withinnate intensity, defying expectations and expanding the scope of heavy music in the process. By following aninternal creative compass, the group have continued to tread a singular path without comparison. Teeteringon an axis of extreme heaviness and transfixing melody, this path reaches another peak on their 2025 EP, AllThat Follows \[SHARPTONE\].Breaking free from expectations and rules, it represents both a signature body of work and a natural stepthrough new territory.“Over the years, we didn’t listen to anyone else, we made the choices we wanted to make, and we believed inthe people who stood behind us,” Brian reflects. “Each day, it’s always just been one foot in front of the other.Since nobody ever thought this would be a viable career option, it’s so meaningful that people want musicfrom us.”Long before tallying hundreds of millions of streams and playing to sold-out crowds, the spark behindCurrents began to flicker as early as 2011. Brian had joined the fold during 2015, and the band pushedforward with a series of critically acclaimed fan favorite releases such as The Place I Feel Safest \[2017\], I LetThe Devil In EP \[2018\], The Way It Ends \[2020\], and The Death We Seek \[2023\]. Following the latter’s release,Everything Is Noise raved, “Currents are one of the few that are capable of crafting a modern metalcore soundthat is unrelenting in every which way whilst still being incredibly accessible.” Beyond praise courtesy ofMetalSucks and more, Distorted Sound rated it “9-out-of-10,” proceeding to profess, “It makes the future ofthis brilliant set of musicians one of the most exciting prospects in music to this day.” Along the way, theyshared stages with everyone from Parkway Drive and Beartooth to Ice Nine Kills, We Came As Romans, andMiss May I.In between non-stop touring, All That Follows came together. Once again, they worked with producer RyanLeitru \[We Came As Romans, Wolves At The Gate\] on a handful of tracks, while Brian and Chris co-producedthe rest of the material.“For the EP, we really compiled some of our strongest songs that made sense together,” he goes on.Currents teased this era with “It Only Gets Darker,” which instantly reeled in over 1.9 million Spotify streams.Among various highlights, “My Severance” evinces the band’s penchant for dynamics. Icy beeps pulsate acrossa violent chugging groove punctured by an ethereal arpeggio and glimmering harmonics. It climaxes on acathartic clean chorus, “Locked in isolation, thrown away. This is my severance.” “It discusses how we’re taken advantage of by people who think they know better because they have moremoney than God and make whatever decisions they want,” he reveals. “They gain control of the governmentby lobbying. We have no way of combatting it unless we’re all together. So, you renounce your faith in theinstitution and stand up to it.”“Making Circles” slips from a wave of guttural distortion into the undertow of fragile verses. The tensionmoves cyclically in a celestial rhythm as Brian sets the scene, “Making circles in the dark to find we’re clouded;we’re lost. No use counting on the stars to guide us over it, this constant motion.”On the other end of the spectrum, “Can’t Turn Back” finds Currents in a crushing pocket. Guttural growls grateup against screeching guitar and a rumbling groove. Vitriol practically seeps through Brian’s words on hisscreams, “Hit the floor. What the fuck do you take me for?” All hell breaks loose, summoning a proclamationright from the gut, “Bow your head to this kingdom.”“It’s a betrayal song,” he sighs. “It’s directed at a person who believes he’s on top of the world, but he doesn’trealize he’s sowing his own fate.”The finale “Rise & Fall” revolves around a riff call-and-response, losing ground in a torrent of propulsivedrums. The hook holds onto the vestiges of a crumbling institution codified in the refrain, “I can’t find what’sleft for me besides another rise and fall. It took all that’s left of me and doubt is still in the way.” Emotionseethes on the bridge until a final bludgeoning breakdown.“It ends with a bunch of questions,” he states. “Is this going to happen to us? What becomes of this? We’verenounced our faith, but how does it end? Is there a second coming, the apocalypse, the singularity, or theafterlife? You don’t know how it’s going to shake out. There is only the rise and fall.”However, Currents are still in the midst of their own quiet rise, and it’s not stopping.“For the most part, I take the worst things I can think of and put them in the music,” he concludes. “I pour alot of hatred, sadness, frustration, confusion, and betrayal into Currents. What comes out is the opposite ofwhat goes in though. When we play a show, people lose their minds, and they’re smiling. We’re trying toprovide a healthy outlet.” ERRA Hailing from Birmingham, Alabama - ERRA has continuously brought titanic riffs, enchanting melodies, and immersive soundscapes to each of their progressive metal albums. They have been described as part mind-altering substance, part meditation, reveling in each musical moment of exultation. ERRA regularly appears on tastemaker playlists like Kickass Metal on Spotify and has charted multiple times on the Billboard’s Heatseekers Albums at No. 1. Most recently ERRA’s sixth album, CURE, strengthens and deepens the forward-thinking progressive metalcore they built their reputation on. It’s an album full of atmospheric ecstasy and moody groove on the heels of their stunning 2021 self-titled set. CURE charts an ambitious course for ERRA’s next era. They’ve toured extensively, with bands including Bad Omens, TesseracT, Ice Nine Kills, Dance Gavin Dance, August Burns Red, and Wage War and continually headline their own sold out tours all across the world.

item.title: Queer Munch
item.description:
Tuesday, April 7, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: a/stir, 818 E Pike St, Seattle, WA

Join us for a vibrant, feel-good Queer Munch at a/stir—a space to gather, celebrate, and connect over great food and even better company. Whether you’re coming with friends, a partner, or flying solo, this is your invitation to show up exactly as you are and be part of a welcoming, affirming community. Expect a delicious gluten-free menu, tasty drinks, and a warm, inclusive atmosphere filled with laughter, conversation, and queer joy. We’ll have music, mingling, and plenty of opportunities to meet new people and build connections. ✨ All identities and expressions are celebrated 🥂 Food \+ drinks available for purchase 🌈 Come as you are

item.title: Make a difference for cats and dogs in need!
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: Pawsitive Alliance, 14150 NE 20th St. F1-233, Bellevue, WA

Are you interested in using your time, talent, and energy to support the vision of a healthy and happy home for every cat and dog in Washington? If yes, then please consider joining our Board! Pawsitive Alliance, a volunteer driven tax-exempt nonprofit organization, is having an open call for Board Members. Founded in 2005, the mission of Pawsitive Alliance is to help end the euthanasia of adoptable dogs and cats in Washington by increasing adoptions, supporting spay and neuter programs, and improving pet retention. Pawsitive Alliance advances are mission through our three programs: -  Adoption program: \#WhyNotMePets campaign, highlighting long-term homeless pets, and supporting adoption events to help homeless finds homes -  Spay/Neuter: providing vouchers and financial and logistical support to increase surgeries and access to end pet overpopulation and suffering -  Pet retention: collecting and distributing pet food and supplies and offering pet ID tags and microchips to keep pets thriving with their caretakers Our programs and activities prioritize increasing services for pets, people, and places across Washington state with few resources. Pawsitive Alliance’s biggest strengths are marketing, organizing, fundraising, and collaborating.  We work with more than 50 human and animal-welfare partners each year. Since 2005, we have helped over 8,000 animals find homes, delivered over 170,000 pounds of donated pet food, and facilitated more than 11,000 spay and neuter surgeries. The Pawsitive Alliance Board is a hands-on group of committed individuals, investing their time and resources to support our mission.  As we celebrate our 20th anniversary in 2025, and look to the future, we are looking to add individuals from diverse backgrounds to join the Board and help the organization’s continued success and growth.  Ideal candidates are committed to our mission, can commit at least 10-15 per month to the organization, are able to represent the organization in the community, are ready to engage in strategy setting, fundraising and networking, and bring a well-rounded set of skills to a unique and hard-working team.​ Pawsitive Alliance is always interested in bringing greater diversity and new areas of expertise, energy, and skills to the Board. For more information on the organization, refer to our website at www.pawsitivealliance.org.  Please fill out our Board Application if you share our passion for helping to end pet homelessness in Washington and are interested in serving and helping end pet overpopulation. Those interested in applying should email our Board Recruitment Committee at info@pawsitivealliance.org.

item.title: Livestream-Public Employees Retirement System (PERS) Retirement Workshop
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: University of Washington, Seattle, WA

Online Wednesday, Apr 8, 2026, 9 a.m. - 12 p.m. PDT Who should attend: Employees currently participating in a PERS, TRS, SERS or LEOFF Retirement Plan and who expect to retire within the next 12 months. Please contact the Department of Retirement Systems \(DRS\) at to request your retirement estimate and application or visit www.drs.wa.gov . This 3-hour workshop is designed for those retiring within the year and covers the following topics: Retirement eligibility Completing the DRS and UW retirement applications Retiree medical plan choices and enrollment Applying for Medicare Leave balances and VEBA, if applicable Notifying your department about your separation Working after retirement Campus room: Benefits Conference Center Accessibility Contact: benefits@uw.edu Event Types: Workshops Event sponsors: UW Human Resources Benefits Office More info: ucs.admin.uw.edu…

item.title: Moomins' Sea Adventures & Tove and the Sea
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - All Events

In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

Event details

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

Event details

item.title: Moomins' Sea Adventures & Tove and the Sea
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: National Nordic Museum, 2655 NW Market Street, Seattle, WA

Dive into the whimsical world of Moominvalley and the life of its creator, Tove Jansson, with *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* and *Tove and the Sea*. *Moomins’ Sea Adventures* brings Moominvalley to life with illustrations and life-sized characters from the stories. In the center of the exhibit is an interactive island complete with a lighthouse and rowboats that invites visitors to imagine adventures of their own. *Tove and the Sea* offers a more intimate counterpoint, tracing Tove Jansson’s lifelong relationship with the Finnish archipelago through large-scale photographs and reflective texts.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

Event details

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

From SEAtoday - Community & Civic

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

Event details

item.title: Boren Banner Series: Camille Trautman
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Frye Art Museum, 704 Terry Ave, Seattle, WA

Every photograph is a reminder that the act of framing is never neutral. In Camille Trautman’s first solo museum exhibition in their hometown, the Seattle-born Duwamish artist uses photography and video to challenge colonial narratives and counter Indigenous erasure. The exhibition presents selections from their ongoing series The North American LCD—spectral self-portraits staged in varied natural landscapes. Rooted in Trautman’s process of coming out as transgender, the images show the artist’s body partially obscured by large LCD screens. The series examines how both landscape photography and digital media can shape or distort cultural identity, often by promoting or denying visibility.

From SEAtoday - All Events

Event details

item.title: Mary Jane
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Leo K. Theater, 155 Mercer St., Seattle, WA

Mary Jane is a cheerful caregiver and unflagging advocate for her toddler, Alex, who lives with cerebral palsy and chronic illness—but the American healthcare system can wear anybody down, especially a single parent. While navigating her son’s health challenges, Mary Jane meets and builds community with women from all walks of life, experiencing joy and connection amidst the distress and heartbreak. Poignantly humorous and deeply cathartic, this semi-autobiographical drama by Tony-nominated playwright Amy Herzog is a love letter to caregivers and support systems of all kinds. By Amy Herzog Directed by Allison Narver

item.title: Chamber 101
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 3:30 PM

Location: Chamber Office, 3100 S 176th St, SeaTac, WA

Chamber 101: An Introduction to Your Local Chamber of Commerce Chamber 101 is an informative and engaging session designed to help members, prospective members, and community partners understand what the Chamber of Commerce is, how it operates, and how it supports local businesses. During this event, attendees will learn: What a Chamber of Commerce is and the role it plays in strengthening the local business ecosystem. What the Chamber does, including advocacy, networking, business development, community partnerships, and economic growth initiatives. How membership works, what benefits are available, and how to make the most of the resources, programs, and visibility opportunities offered by the Chamber. Ways to get involved, from attending events and committees to leveraging promotional tools and support services. Chamber 101 is ideal for anyone looking to better understand how the Chamber supports business success and how to fully engage with its mission and network. This session provides a welcoming space to ask questions, meet Chamber staff, and connect with fellow community members.

item.title: SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Book-It Repertory Theatre, 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, WA

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

Event details

item.title: Tai Chi for Health and Balance
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview Tai Chi is a health sustaining exercise and a tool for improved body awareness. Practiced by the elderly for balance and circulation and the young for relaxation and martial arts, it is a wonderful holistic activity for all ages. We'll focus on learning the entire Yang short form, and you'll receive guidance in developing your own regular practice at home. During this class, you'll learn: -The Yang Tai Chi short form, which is comprised of 35 individual movements -Chi Kung, a warm-up exercise and relaxation activity with 18 movements -Elements of Chinese Medical Philosophy, such as Yin/Yang theory and Five Elements transformation -the basics of Taoist philosophy Learning Objectives Learn basic warm-up stretches to improve your overall movement Discover the Yang-style short form of Tai Chi Practice the Yang-style under the direction of an expert instructor Gain knowledge about the history and background of Tai Chi Who Might Enjoy This Class? Individuals seeking a low-impact exercise routine Anyone interested in improving their coordination and balance People interested in developing their focus, relaxation and overall wellness

item.title: 5-Course Gil Family Estates Winemaker Dinner at 1 Hotel Seattle
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 1 Hotel Seattle, 2125 Terry Avenue, Seattle, WA

Join the 1 Hotel Seattle team at La Loba for a special evening celebrating 110 years of Gil Family Estates with a five-course tasting menu and curated wine pairings. Guests will enjoy wines from across the Gil family portfolio—including selections from Bodegas El Nido, Shaya, and Juan Gil—presented by visiting winemakers and members of the Gil  Each course will be introduced by Chef Oscar and his culinary team, highlighting seasonal flavors designed to complement the wines. The evening begins with a welcome toast and continues through five paired courses in our Emerald Private Dining Room. Seating is communal to encourage conversation and connection among fellow wine lovers.

Event details

item.title: Artist's Way Classes Winter 2026 are Now Open for Registration
item.description:
Wednesday, April 8, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: The Fauntleroy Schoolhouse, 9131 California Avenue SW, Seattle, WA

The Artist's Way Classes for Winter 2026 via Zoom or In-Person are Now Open for Registration The Artist's Way by Julia Cameron is an international bestseller on the subject of creativity. Whether you see yourself as an artist or not, this book and class can be an incredibly useful resource to tap into your creative side which can benefit many parts of your life. Led by Creativity Instructor, Kate Gavigan, the 2 hour a week 12 week long class will take students through the 12 chapters with an emphasis on the accompanying chapter exercises and additional creativity exercises and strategies \(i.e. meditation tools\). This material can work with anyone, no matter how dormant or energized their creative life may be.  Students report that as a result of going through our Artist's Way class they more fully and genuinely know themselves, they discover or re-discover their gifts and talents and have more fun and joy in their lives. To quote Julia, "In order to retrieve your creativity, you need to find it." The basic tools and the additional creativity exercises that we incorporate into the 12 week course can help you do just that. No prerequisites required for this class.  Students also meet with the instructor for a one on one chat on Zoom for 30 minutes prior to the first class and before the last class. Section I: January 19 – April 6 – via Zoom Monday Mornings, 10:00 am – noon $395 \(12 weeks\) OR Section II:  January 19 – April 6 - via Zoom Monday Evenings, 6:30 – 8:30 pm \(Pacific Standard time\) $395 \(12 weeks\) OR Section III:  – January 21 – April 8 via Zoom Wednesdays, 3:00 – 5:00 pm \(Pacific Standard time\) $395 \(12 weeks\) OR Section IV: January 21 – April 8 via In-PERSON Wednesdays, 6:30 – 8:30 pm \(Pacific Standard time\) $395 \(12 weeks\) This In-Person Class held in West Seattle at the Fauntleroy Schoolhouse, 9131 California Ave. SW, Seattle, WA Instructor for all sections: Kate Gavigan Note: Students will use the book The Artist’s Way by Julia Cameron. Students are responsible for purchasing their own copy of The Artist’s Way by Julia Cameron. To Register or for more information: https://seattleartistswaycenter.com/class-offerings For testimonials from past students: https://seattleartistswaycenter.com/class-testimonials Kate Gavigan is passionate about The Artist's Way after offering classes since 2009 and seeing the positive changes in her students' lives and having personally experienced the benefits of the materials. Kate worked in social services as a trainer for over 10 years when she found herself drawn to the material in The Artist's Way, which helped her uncover a passion for theatre and the arts. She credits her mid-life career change \(now working at her dream jobs as an Artist's Way Instructor and Speaker since 2009, a Creativity Coach, a Home Organizer and pursuing her interest in Acting and Music\) to having gone through The Artist's Way.
